summary: Decompiled most of src/melee/pl/plbonus.c's fn_80039618, a large player bonus/stat flagging routine, while refining pl_804D6470_t threshold fields needed by the implementation. The PR also forced fn_80038700 not to inline and changed fn_8003CC84 digit extraction arithmetic, which the bot report says made fn_8003CC84 fully match. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code +2980 bytes and matched data +456 bytes, with plbonus .data and .sdata2 reaching 100% and fn_80039618 improving from 0.00% to 99.67%. There was no substantive PR body or human review feedback in the slice.

key_files.role: Replaced the fn_80039618 asm stub marker with a large C implementation, added dont_inline pragmas around fn_80038700, and rewrote fn_8003CC84 digit extraction.
Refined pl_804D6470_t fields from byte padding to typed float/u32/int threshold fields used by plbonus logic.

what_changed: Implemented fn_80039618 in C, using Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2, Player_GetActionStats, Player_GetEntity, plActionStats counters, stale move table fields, game-mode helpers, and setFlag calls to award many bonus flags.

The new fn_80039618 logic checks hit/attack totals, aerial-only and special-only hits, move index ranges, thrown-item hit ratios, no-attack/no-fall cases, damage thresholds, stale-move table state, opponent damage ratios, and KO distribution conditions.
Wrapped fn_80038700 with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, preserving it as a separate helper for summing counter ranges.
Changed fn_8003CC84 decimal digit extraction from temp_ret % 100 expressions to subtracting the hundreds and tens components, which coincided with the bot reporting fn_8003CC84 as 100% matched.
Updated pl_804D6470_t at offsets x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, and x160 from anonymous byte arrays to typed fields so fn_80039618 could compare against config thresholds naturally.
decomp.dev reported 6 new matches, including main/melee/pl/plbonus .data, .sdata2, and fn_8003CC84, plus fn_80039618 improving to 99.67%.
smart_moves: Kept the large bonus routine conservative: address-style function names and x-offset fields were retained where semantics were not proven, avoiding speculative naming.

Hoisted frequently used stats counters and pointer fields into locals such as hit_counts, atk_counts, x358_counts, attacks_total, hits_total, and x358_total, matching the diff evidence and making the long condition chain tractable.
Used explicit goto labels such as skip_only_low_moves and skip_combo_count for loop early-exit patterns instead of forcing overly structured C that might alter codegen.
Matched likely compiler behavior with a pointer-walk/countdown loop over x358_counts starting at index 1, rather than only using indexed array syntax.
Typed only the pl_804D6470_t offsets needed by the new code, enabling float/u32 comparisons without inventing semantic names for the config table.
Used dont_inline pragmas on the small summing helper fn_80038700, a targeted codegen control that is reusable when a helper must remain a call site for matching.
decomp_lessons: Large decompilation wins often require header work first: replacing padding with correctly typed fields in shared config structs can make a complex C implementation possible.
For MWCC matching, expression spelling matters. The fn_8003CC84 change shows that `% 100` can produce different codegen than subtracting the previously computed hundreds component.
When matching long statistics routines, preserve original loop bounds and index ranges exactly, such as 1..99, 0x40..0x62, 1..0x10, and player loops over 0..5.
Do not be afraid to use limited gotos for compiler-faithful control flow when loops need shared early-exit labels.
Small helper functions may need explicit inlining control; matching one large caller can depend on preventing the compiler from absorbing a short utility routine.

Bot match reports can reveal secondary effects outside directly edited files; here hsd_3AA7 items improved in the report even though the diff only changed plbonus.c and types.h, so future analysis should treat those as reported side effects unless confirmed by diffs.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-style names: fn_80039618, fn_80038700, fn_8003CC84, pl_80038628, pl_80037B2C, and pl_800414C0.
Offset-based config fields in pl_804D6470_t remained xNN names, but were given concrete C types when usage was known.

Local variable names were semi-semantic and counter-oriented, including aerials, atk_thrown, hit_thrown, atk_counts, hit_counts, x358_counts, attacks_total, hits_total, x358_total, found_low, found_high, max_ratio, and sum_ratio.
Existing project type/getter naming was reused: pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, plActionStats, Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2, Player_GetActionStats, Player_GetEntity, and Player_GetKOsByPlayerIndex.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop was used around fn_80038700.
PAD_STACK(16) was placed at the end of fn_80039618 to satisfy stack/codegen requirements.
Goto labels skip_only_low_moves and skip_combo_count were used to preserve loop exit structure.
Pointer iteration with u32* p = &x358_counts[1] and a countdown for loop was used for one count/min/max scan.
Digit extraction in fn_8003CC84 was rewritten as subtraction from temp_ret after computing temp_r5, then subtracting temp_r3_3 * 10, avoiding repeated modulo expressions.
The implementation used explicit casts such as (f32), (u32), (s32), and fn_80038700((int*) stats, ...) to line up comparisons and helper-call signatures.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice, and the PR body was empty.
The only substantive comment was from decomp-dev[bot], reporting matched code 67.85% (+0.08%, +2980 bytes) and matched data 39.68% (+0.04%, +456 bytes).
The bot reported plbonus .data and .sdata2 as new 100% matches, fn_8003CC84 as a new 100% function match, and fn_80039618 improving from 0.00% to 99.67%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate the remaining 0.33% mismatch in fn_80039618 after this PR.
Search for other plbonus digit-extraction or modulo patterns that may need the subtraction form used in fn_8003CC84.
Audit additional pl_804D6470_t xNN fields and rename them only when bonus semantics are proven.
Map setFlag bonus IDs in fn_80039618, such as 0x0, 0x21, 0x95, 0x96, 0xAA, to known in-game bonus names.
Confirm whether the hsd_3AA7 improvements reported by decomp.dev were layout/reporting side effects or caused by broader matching interactions outside the changed diff.
